title: desmodium heterocarpon var. buergeri
intro: Desmodium heterocarpon var. buergeri, also known as small hop tree or Buerguer's tick trefoil, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of this plant include relieving symptoms of menstrual cramps, menopausal hot flashes, and digestive issues, as well as having antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Desmodium heterocarpon var. buergeri are often dried and made into teas or infusions, and can also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance their therapeutic effects.
